paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: In the beginning God created the heavens and the earth.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And the earth was waste and void; and darkness was upon the face of the deep: and the Spirit of God moved upon the face of the waters

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And God said, Let there be light: and there was light.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And God saw the light, that it was good: and God divided the light from the darkness.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And God called the light Day, and the darkness he called Night. And there was evening and there was morning, one day.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And God said, Let there be a firmament in the midst of the waters, and let it divide the waters from the waters.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And God made the firmament, and divided the waters which were under the firmament from the waters which were above the firmament: and it was so.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And God called the firmament Heaven. And there was evening and there was morning, a second day.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And God said, Let the waters under the heavens be gathered together unto one place, and let the dry land appear: and it was so.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And God called the dry land Earth; and the gathering together of the waters called he Seas: and God saw that it was good.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And God said, Let the earth put forth grass, herbs yielding seed, [and] fruit-trees bearing fruit after their kind, wherein is the seed thereof, upon the earth: and it was so.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And the earth brought forth grass, herbs yielding seed after their kind, and trees bearing fruit, wherein is the seed thereof, after their kind: and God saw that it was good.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And there was evening and there was morning, a third day.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And God said, Let there be lights in the firmament of heaven to divide the day from the night; and let them be for signs, and for seasons, and for days and years:

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: and let them be for lights in the firmament of heaven to give light upon the earth: and it was so.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And God made the two great lights; the greater light to rule the day, and the lesser light to rule the night: [he made] the stars also.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And God set them in the firmament of heaven to give light upon the earth,

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: and to rule over the day and over the night, and to divide the light from the darkness: and God saw that it was good.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And there was evening and there was morning, a fourth day.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And God said, Let the waters swarm with swarms of living creatures, and let birds fly above the earth in the open firmament of heaven.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And God created the great sea-monsters, and every living creature that moveth, wherewith the waters swarmed, after their kind, and every winged bird after its kind: and God saw that it was good.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And God blessed them, saying, Be fruitful, and multiply, and fill the waters in the seas, and let birds multiply on the earth.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And there was evening and there was morning, a fifth day.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And God said, Let the earth bring forth living creatures after their kind, cattle, and creeping things, and beasts of the earth after their kind: and it was so.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And God made the beasts of the earth after their kind, and the cattle after their kind, and everything that creepeth upon the ground after its kind: and God saw that it was good.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And God said, Let us make man in our image, after our likeness: and let them have dominion over the fish of the sea, and over the birds of the heavens, and over the cattle, and over all the earth, and over every creeping thing that creepeth upon the earth.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And God created man in his own image, in the image of God created he him; male and female created he them.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And God blessed them: and God said unto them, Be fruitful, and multiply, and replenish the earth, and subdue it; and have dominion over the fish of the sea, and over the birds of the heavens, and over every living thing that moveth upon the earth.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And God said, Behold, I have given you every herb yielding seed, which is upon the face of all the earth, and every tree, in which is the fruit of a tree yielding seed; to you it shall be for food:

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: and to every beast of the earth, and to every bird of the heavens, and to everything that creepeth upon the earth, wherein there is life, [I have given] every green herb for food: and it was so.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And God saw everything that he had made, and, behold, it was very good. And there was evening and there was morning, the sixth day.
